persistence_basis: ADHD symptom and cognitive benefit hold only while medicated; randomized discontinuation produces faster/greater relapse than staying on drug; in the MTA the 14-month medication advantage was no longer apparent by 36 months
persistence_caveat: symptomatic + tolerance (stimulant effects wane over years); non-ADHD enhancement is smaller, task-dependent and equally reversible
persistence_source: Molina 2009 JAACAP (MTA 36-mo); randomized-discontinuation studies of dexmethylphenidate / methylphenidate-LA / lisdexamfetamine / guanfacine-ER

persistence_basis: reopens adult juvenile-like (critical-period) plasticity and promotes hippocampal neurogenesis + BDNF/TrkB signalling (iPlasticity); induced visual-cortex plasticity can outlast the natural critical period and some cellular dematuration is reported after withdrawal in rodents
persistence_caveat: a PERMISSIVE window - durable change is contingent on paired experience/training (e.g. fluoxetine + extinction), not a standalone boost; rodent work at supra-clinical doses; no human cognitive-enhancement persistence shown
persistence_source: Maya-Vetencourt 2008 Science; Steinzeig 2019 Eur J Neurosci; Castren iPlasticity reviews

persistence_basis: selective alpha-2A agonist that strengthens prefrontal working-memory circuits (Arnsten), but guanfacine-ER is in the same discontinuation dataset showing rapid relapse off-drug
persistence_caveat: mechanistically interesting yet still symptomatic; benefit reverses on stopping
persistence_source: Arnsten alpha-2A PFC work; guanfacine-ER discontinuation studies

persistence_basis: peripherally-restricted kappa-opioid agonist (uremic pruritus), engineered not to cross the BBB
persistence_caveat: no CNS action; routing artifact

persistence_basis: SNRI antidepressant; mood/secondary effects reverse on washout; no established durable cognitive enhancement
persistence_caveat: routed to catecholaminergic by aryloxypropanamine scaffold; symptomatic

persistence_basis: MAO-B inhibitor; the DATATOP / Sano lineage delayed functional endpoints in AD/PD but did not durably enhance cognition
persistence_caveat: disease-course modification not established; functional not cognitive endpoints

persistence_basis: antianginal, withdrawn for torsades risk
persistence_caveat: routing artifact; withdrawn drug

persistence_basis: obsolete short-acting muscle relaxant
persistence_caveat: routing artifact

persistence_basis: antimuscarinic antispasmodic; chronic anticholinergic burden is itself linked to higher dementia risk
persistence_caveat: opposes cognition; routing artifact
persistence_source: anticholinergic cognitive-burden literature (Gray 2015 JAMA Intern Med)

persistence_basis: NSAID; the ADAPT RCT of naproxen/celecoxib did not improve cognition (weak detrimental signal for naproxen); ibufenac itself was withdrawn for hepatotoxicity
persistence_caveat: contested AD-prevention hypothesis that failed its RCT; not enhancement; withdrawn drug

persistence_basis: antiepileptic carrying a black-box warning (aplastic anaemia / hepatic failure); not a wake-promoting agent
persistence_caveat: routing artifact; serious toxicity

persistence_basis: sedating first-generation antihistamine with anticholinergic activity
persistence_caveat: sedating - the opposite of wake-promoting; the 'wake_promoting' label here is structural not pharmacological

persistence_basis: cholinesterase inhibitors are approved for symptomatic management of Alzheimer's; they do not alter disease course and the symptomatic benefit is lost on discontinuation
persistence_caveat: speculative preclinical neuroprotection for the class is not clinically established; no durable enhancement in healthy people
persistence_source: AD treatment guidelines; cholinesterase-inhibitor deprescribing literature

persistence_basis: topical antifungal (tinea versicolor); not a systemic CNS agent
persistence_caveat: pure routing artifact

persistence_basis: ADAGIO delayed-start: rasagiline 1 mg/day met all three hierarchical endpoints (consistent with a disease-modifying effect) but 2 mg/day did not - a dose-inconsistency that undercuts interpretation; endpoints were motor (UPDRS), cognition was a tiny mentation sub-score
persistence_caveat: equivocal; motor not cognitive; field consensus is that MAO-B inhibitors have not clearly altered Parkinson's course

persistence_basis: bis-quaternary AChE inhibitor used as an ophthalmic glaucoma agent; BBB-impermeant
persistence_caveat: no CNS action; routing artifact

persistence_basis: mu-opioid agonist; chronic opioid exposure is associated with cognitive impairment plus tolerance and dependence
persistence_caveat: safety inversion - potential lasting harm, not enhancement

persistence_basis: quaternary-ammonium AChE inhibitor; permanent positive charge means it does not cross the blood-brain barrier (used for myasthenia and reversal of neuromuscular blockade)
persistence_caveat: no central cognitive action regardless of scaffold similarity to galantamine; pure routing artifact

persistence_basis: mu-opioid agonist; chronic use impairs cognition
persistence_caveat: safety inversion
